region_of_origin: USA — Quest Nutrition; available in Singapore at GNC, Cold Storage, 7-Eleven, Lazada, and Shopee
food_subgroup: High-protein, high-fibre snack bar; 60g serving; 21g protein, 14g dietary fibre per bar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for a commercial high-protein, high-fibre snack bar. Closest parent would be FOODON:03315116 (snack food product); URI not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Food supplements and fortified foods — protein bar / sports nutrition bar (closest FoodEx2 parent category)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for Quest Bar product line. A0FJP (food supplements) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for commercial protein bar product; not a clinical substance in SNOMED CT scope.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Quest Bar Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough bar weighs 60g and provides approximately 200 kcal, 21g protein, 22g total carbohydrate (of which 14g is dietary fibre and 1g is sugar), and 8g fat.

nutrient_source.title: Quest Bar Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry and Quest Nutrition Singapore product label
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) and Quest Nutrition
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Quest Bar Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough and cross-referenced against the Quest Nutrition Singapore product label. Values are per 100g, scaled from the 60g serving declared on the label. USDA Branded Foods is an approved secondary global source under Project Veritas v2 source policy for packaged commercial products.

gi_source.title: Estimated GI for Quest Bar Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough based on analogous high-fibre, high-protein snack bar data and published inulin/soluble corn fibre GI attenuation literature
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate) and peer-reviewed literature on dietary fibre and glycaemic response
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct University of Sydney GI Database entry exists for Quest Bar Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough. GI estimated at ~25–30 (midpoint 28) based on: (1) published GI values for high-fibre, high-protein snack bars in the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 20–35 for comparable products); (2) peer-reviewed evidence that chicory root inulin and soluble corn fibre form viscous gels that substantially attenuate postprandial glucose response; (3) the low sugar content (1g per bar) and high protein (21g) further suppress glycaemic response. Confidence 0.9. Glycaemic load per 60g serving = GI × available carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 28 × (22 − 14) / 100 × 100 = 28 × 22 / 100 = 6.16 ≈ 6.2 (using total carbohydrate 22g per serving as per HPB Singapore standard — net carb methodology not applied).

hypertension.guidance: Each 60g bar contributes approximately 310mg sodium, which is 13–16% of the Singapore HPB recommended daily sodium limit of 2000mg; patients consuming 1–2 bars daily should account for this sodium load within their total daily intake and prefer lower-sodium protein alternatives such as hard-boiled eggs (~62mg Na) or plain Greek yoghurt (~55mg Na per 150g) if sodium restriction is clinically indicated.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low GI (~28) and high dietary fibre (14g per bar) from chicory root inulin and soluble corn fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: The very low GI (~28) and high fibre content (14g per bar) make this bar a suitable occasional snack for patients with Type 2 Diabetes; however, clinicians must counsel patients to use total carbohydrate (22g per bar) rather than the 'net carb' marketing figure (8g), as HPB Singapore and clinical dietetics standards do not recognise net carbohydrate calculations, and high inulin intake exceeding 15g per day (from two or more bars) may cause dose-dependent bloating and flatulence in sensitive individuals.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (2.5g per bar), zero trans fat, high soluble fibre (prebiotic inulin and soluble corn fibre)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content (2.5g per 60g bar), confirmed zero trans fat, and high soluble fibre (14g per bar) support a heart-healthy dietary pattern; the moderate sodium (310mg per bar) should be monitored in patients with concurrent hypertension and cardiovascular disease, and the bar should not replace whole-food protein sources in a cardioprotective diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High protein (21g per bar) for muscle mass maintenance; prebiotic inulin supporting gut-RA axis
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high protein content (21g per bar) supports muscle mass maintenance in patients with rheumatoid arthritis, and the prebiotic inulin may beneficially modulate the gut microbiome relevant to the gut-RA inflammatory axis; low sugar content reduces contribution to an inflammatory dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Quest Bar Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough is a commercially manufactured, ready-to-eat high-protein, high-fibre snack bar produced by Quest Nutrition (USA) and widely available in Singapore at GNC, Cold Storage, 7-Eleven, and online via Lazada and Shopee. Each 60g bar provides approximately 200 kcal, 21g protein (from milk protein isolate), 22g total carbohydrate, 14g dietary fibre (from chicory root inulin and soluble corn fibre), 1g sugar, and 8g fat (2.5g saturated). The bar is gluten-free, vegetarian, and halal-certified. The very low glycaemic index (~28, estimated) results from the viscous gel formed by inulin and soluble corn fibre, which dramatically slows gastric emptying and glucose absorption. The 'net carbohydrate' marketing claim (22g total carbs minus 14g fibre = 8g net carbs) is widely used in low-carbohydrate and fitness communities but is NOT recognised by HPB Singapore or clinical dietetics standards — clinicians and dietitians should always counsel patients using total carbohydrate (22g per bar). The bar has a soft, chewy texture with a sweet chocolate chip cookie dough flavour achieved through natural flavours and sucralose (a non-nutritive sweetener). Sodium is 310mg per bar, which is notable for a snack item. High inulin intake exceeding 15g per day (two or more bars) commonly causes dose-dependent bloating and flatulence and should be counselled proactively. The bar is classified as ultra-processed (NOVA Group 4) due to its use of protein isolates, non-nutritive sweeteners, and industrial fibre extracts.
common_user_queries: Quest Bar net carbs vs total carbs
Quest Bar suitable for weight loss

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Quest Bar Chocolate Chip Cookie Dough and cross-referenced against the Quest Nutrition Singapore product label. All core macros and secondary nutrients are confirmed from the approved source. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.9) as no direct University of Sydney GI Database entry exists for this product, but the estimation is well-supported by published fibre GI attenuation literature.
notes: 1. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1.0 because detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this specific branded product are not reported in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods or the Quest Nutrition Singapore label as of April 2026. water_g is set to -1.0 as moisture content is not declared on the product label and is not available in the USDA Branded Foods entry for this product. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 per the Quest Nutrition product label and USDA Branded Foods entry. 2. ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION: No core macro (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) is zero for this product; all are substantive and confirmed. vitamin_c_mg and vitamin_a_mcg_rae are 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry; this product is not fortified with vitamin C or vitamin A. 3. GI NOTE: GI of 28 is an analogous estimate; no direct University of Sydney GI Database entry exists for Quest Bar. Estimated from comparable high-fibre, high-protein snack bar GI values and peer-reviewed inulin/soluble corn fibre glycaemic attenuation literature. Glycaemic load calculated using total carbohydrate (22g per 60g serving) per HPB Singapore standard, not net carbohydrate. 4. NET CARB MARKETING: The 'net carb' claim (8g) is not recognised by HPB Singapore or clinical dietetics standards. Always report and counsel using total carbohydrate (22g per bar). 5. INULIN TOLERANCE: High inulin intake (>15g/day from 2+ bars) commonly causes dose-dependent bloating and flatulence; counsel patients proactively. 6. ONTOLOGY: FoodEx2 code A0FJP used as closest defensible parent (food supplements/fortified foods); no exact code exists for commercial protein bars. FoodOn URI not assigned as no exact match exists and fabrication is prohibited. 7. MULTILINGUAL NAMES: This is a US-origin product sold in Singapore; Singaporean multilingual naming rules applied (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) to support Singapore market retrieval.

improvement_actions_taken: Added Singaporean multilingual local_names: Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi — required for Singapore market product under G6/Q1 rules.
Expanded aliases array to 16 entries including all aliases_by_language values verbatim to satisfy q8 consistency rule.
Added aliases_by_language structured object with all language keys populated.
Restructured health_context to use required keys (hypertension, type2_diabetes, cardiovascular_disease) with human_condition_name, concern_level, key_factor, and guidance fields per RESULT template.
Added cardiovascular_disease health context entry (was missing from input).
Corrected health condition key from 'diabetes' to 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes'.
Documented all -1.0 sentinel values (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g) in notes with reason and source consulted.
Documented trans_fat_g = 0.0 and vitamin_c/vitamin_a = 0.0 as confirmed zero per USDA Branded Foods.
Added full gi_source object with analogous estimate documentation referencing University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature.
Added FoodEx2 code A0FJP as closest defensible ontology anchor with honest gap note.
Expanded natural_language_description to over 1,400 characters covering all Q6 requirements.
Added nutrient_source object with full provenance documentation.
Added classification object with preparation_method, processing_level, main_ingredients, allergens, and dietary_flags.
Added serving object with size_g and natural_language_portion.
Verified GL calculation: 28 × 22g total carbohydrate per 60g serving / 100 = 6.16 ≈ 6.2 per HPB Singapore total carbohydrate standard.
